author: allnes
title: TensorFlow layer tests fail on CPU FP16 - Rank, MatrixBandPart, Pooling 5D, RaggedTensorToTensor
signature: tensorflow layer tests fail cpu fp16 precision test failures matrixbandpart rank pooling ragged tensor|Flaky Test

failed_jobs.name: TensorFlow Layer Tests / TensorFlow Layer Tests
failed_jobs.symptom: 4 TF layer tests FAILED on CPU/FP16; 20 additional ERRORs from worker crashes; log ends at 66% progress

key_errors: [gw2] [ 61%] FAILED .../test_tf_MatrixBandPart.py::TestMatrixBandPart::test_matrix_band_part_basic[ ie_device:CPU - precision:FP16 - num_upper:1 - num_lower:-4 - input_type:<class 'numpy.float32'> - input_shape:[3, 4, 4] ]
root_cause: All 4 pytest-xdist workers (gw0-gw3) reported a test FAILED simultaneously at the 61% mark. Each worker was running a different TF layer test (Rank, MatrixBandPart, Pooling 5D, RaggedTensorToTensor), all using CPU device with FP16 precision. After the FAILED reports, each worker generated repeated ERROR status for subsequent tests, indicating worker process crashes after the initial failures. The test failures appear to be pre-existing numerical accuracy or precision issues in CPU FP16 path, unrelated to PR #37027 which only modifies GPU plugin clang-tidy configuration. avg-pool VALID padding on a large 5D tensor [1,3,224,224,224] passed with SAME padding but failed with VALID, suggesting a numerical precision regression in the CPU FP16 pooling path.
reproduction_steps: Run: python3 -m pytest /path/to/layer_tests/tensorflow_tests/test_tf_Pooling.py::TestPooling::test_pool_5D -v -k 'FP16 and VALID and avg' --ie_device CPU
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed TF layer tests to determine if this is truly flaky or a persistent regression
Investigate if any recent changes to the CPU FP16 path could affect Pooling/MatrixBandPart/Rank/RaggedTensor operations
If confirmed persistent, file a bug for CPU FP16 numerical failures in these ops
Re-add PR #37027 to the merge queue since it is a GPU-only clang-tidy change unrelated to these failures
prevention_strategies: Add explicit FP16 tolerance markers to tests known to be numerically sensitive on CPU; consider separate CI stages for GPU vs CPU changes to avoid false blockers from unrelated test suites.
historical_context: First occurrence of this specific TF FP16 test failure pattern in the CI Doctor MQ database.
